sourceMovie.title: Hidden Figures
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Hidden Figures for the same reason it broke through in 2016: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a history rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Theodore Melfi keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: First Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hidden Figures clicked for you, First Man (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hidden Figures?
faq.answer: Try First Man, Elvis, Richard Jewell—each shares DNA with Hidden Figures while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hidden Figures?
faq.answer: Hidden Figures (2016) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hidden Figures worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
